disagreements.agent_clean: どの製品も, ISO で定められたオープンソースライセンスによって同じ条件で提供されている。
disagreements.proposed: どの製品も, OSI で定められたオープンソースライセンスによって同じ条件で提供されている。
garble_found: ISO0 → ISO (spurious trailing digit 0 removed; source shows only three letters I,S,O followed by a space)
disagreements.notes: Read page-44.png and made high-magnification crops (crop_osi_big.png, crop_letters_big.png). Choice ウ source unambiguously reads "ISO" (serif capital I, then S, then round O) + half-width space + "で定められたオープンソースライセンスによって同じ条件で提供されている。". The OCR "ISO0" appended a spurious digit 0 that is absent from the image — removed it. Kept the half-width space "ISO で" matching the visible gap, and the ASCII ", " comma per corpus style. meaning_consistent=false: the source literally says "ISO" while both zh/en reference translations say "OSI (Open Source Initiative)". The translator appears to have corrected ISO→OSI (logical, since OSI defines open source licenses), but the authoritative source image clearly states ISO and the task forbids paraphrasing to match the translation, so clean_jp preserves "ISO". This acronym mismatch should be flagged for the translation, not patched into the source transcription.

fp_results.reasoning: The zero_in_alpha pattern fired on "RAID0" and "RAID1" in stem_jp and all four choices. These are legitimate RAID level names, not OCR garble. The question (令和5年 問63) asks about usable storage when two 500GB HDDs are configured as RAID0 (striping → 1TB) and RAID1 (mirroring → 500GB), with correct_answer イ. The Japanese stem/choices, the zh translation, and the en translation all intentionally keep "RAID0"/"RAID1" — confirming the digits are part of the technical term. Nothing should be altered.

The "zero_in_alpha" pattern fired on ESSID strings A0B1C2D3E4 / A0B1C2D3E5 / A0B1C2D3E6 in choices ア-エ of data/ip/quiz/questions.json. These are legitimate, deliberately-constructed alphanumeric ESSID identifiers for a wireless-LAN question (stem asks which ESSID + self-IP combination to set on the current and old PC). The 0s are an intrinsic part of the hex-style identifier, and the values are internally consistent across all four choices and load-bearing for the correct answer イ (matching ESSID A0B1C2D3E4 on both PCs with distinct IPs .2/.3). Not OCR garble; must not be altered. The zh/en translations would carry the identical identifier, so no field needs changing.

The flagged field is the reconstructed stem (stem_jp_clean and the zh/en stem translations), which ends with the flow-diagram line "[　　] → [ A ] → [　　] → [　　]". The detector's interior_fw_space pattern fired on the full-width spaces inside the empty boxes [　　].

Verification: (1) The raw stem_jp in questions.json is itself clean prose with no boxes — only the reconstructed *_clean / translation fields carry the diagram line. (2) The source page (data/ip/exams/pages/2009h21a/page-16.png, 問44) shows a real flowchart of four rectangles joined by arrows: empty → [A] → empty → empty. The textual reconstruction [　　] → [ A ] → [　　] → [　　] is a faithful rendering of that figure, with full-width spaces standing in for the three empty boxes and [ A ] for the labeled box. (3) Semantics check out: flow is 変更要求の受付 → 変更内容の評価(A) → 変更の指示 → 変更の反映, so A = 変更内容の評価 = choice ア, matching correct_answer.

The full-width spaces are intentional, legitimate flow-diagram-box content (the exact "[　　]" pattern called out as a known false-positive class), not trailing junk or OCR garble. It must not be altered.

The detector's "trailing_junk" pattern fired because stem_jp_clean ends in "]". Verified against the source image data/ip/exams/pages/2013h25h/page-31.png: 問74 contains a network-topology flow diagram [ブラウザ] ― [プロキシサーバ] ― [ルータ] ― (インターネット) ― [Webサーバ]. The reconstructed stem (stem_jp = "図の構成で…通信内容が暗号化される区間はどれか。", plus the appended diagram line) faithfully reproduces this diagram, and the trailing "]" is the legitimate closing bracket of the diagram box [Webサーバ]. The zh/en stems mirror it ([浏览器]…[Web 服务器] / [Browser]…[Web server]) and the choices reference プロキシサーバ/ルータ/Webサーバ/ブラウザ, all consistent with the diagram boxes. This is exactly the flow-diagram-box exception (not OCR garble); the text must not be altered.

The flagged stem is a legitimate IPA option-list with full-width spacing: "a　アルバイト　　b　契約社員　　c　派遣社員　　d　パートタイマ". The interior_fw_space pattern is triggered by intentional full-width-space delimiters that separate the labeled list items a/b/c/d — standard IPA exam formatting. Each label maps to a real, complete Japanese employment-form term, and choices_jp (a,b / a,b,d / b / b,c) reference these items coherently, confirming the list is intact. No scrambled or nonsensical characters present. Must NOT be altered.

The interior_fw_space hits are legitimate IPA formatting, not OCR garble. The 16 full-width spaces (U+3000) are either (a) standard list/section-header indents — 〔テクノロジ〕\n問87　, (1)　, (2)　, (3)　 — or (b) fill-in-the-blank placeholders 　　a　　 and 　　b　　. The stem explicitly asks 'a，bに入れる字句の適切な組合せはどれか' (which words fill blanks a and b), so the spaced a/b are the answer blanks. The trilingual translations deliberately preserve the same placeholders (zh 对它们　　a　　, en ___a___) and the curated stem_jp_clean keeps 　　a　　 / 　　b　　 intact. These are equivalent to the known-legitimate [　　] flow/blank boxes and must not be altered.

The detector scans the DISPLAYED stem, which for this question is stem_jp_clean (present in translations/2016h28h.json). That clean stem reconstructs the figure as a flow diagram and ends in "[ a ] → [網掛け] → [網掛け] → [ 運用 ] → [網掛け]". The trailing_junk regex matched the final "]" (confirmed snippet "]"), but that "]" is the legitimate closing bracket of the flow-diagram box [網掛け] (shaded) — exactly the protected "flow-diagram box" class. The raw stem_jp's real OCR junk ("\n|・") is NOT what was flagged and is not displayed. Altering the clean stem's trailing "]" would corrupt a legitimate diagram box. Therefore this is a false positive and must not be changed.
